topic: Feynman Lectures on Physics Volume I Part VI (Chapters 41-52): Brownian Motion, Thermodynamics, Sound, Waves, and Symmetry. Topics: The Brownian movement (random walk, Einstein-Smoluchowski, fluctuation-dissipation), applications of kinetic theory (transport phenomena, thermionic emission, evaporation/condensation rates), diffusion (Fick's laws, random walk connection, diffusion equation), the laws of thermodynamics (first and second laws, Carnot cycle, entropy), illustrations of thermodynamics (internal energy, Helmholtz/Gibbs free energies, phase transitions), ratchet and pawl as a Maxwell's demon thought experiment (Feynman's famous analysis of why it doesn't work), sound and the wave equation (longitudinal waves, speed of sound, standing waves), beats (superposition, group velocity), modes (normal modes, Fourier analysis), harmonics (musical instruments, Fourier series), waves (traveling waves, dispersion, KdV/mathematical notes), symmetry in physical laws (Noether's theorem intuition, conservation laws from symmetry, CPT). Deep research into Feynman's pedagogical approach culminating in the elegant final chapter on symmetry at Caltech 1963.
